channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Lidl discloses online shop breach after service provider hack
item.description: German discount supermarket chain Lidl notified customers in Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands that attackers stole their personal information in a breach at a service provider. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Breach at the Beach: Play the Ultimate Entra ID CTF
item.description: Learn how attackers abuse Entra ID through a free hands-on Capture the Flag. Varonis created the Breach at the Beach CTF to teach defenders how to investigate Entra ID attack techniques using realisti...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UK charges suspects linked to Russian Coms call spoofing platform
item.description: UK authorities charged five people following a National Crime Agency (NCA) investigation into Russian Coms, a major caller ID spoofing platform used by criminals to make over 1.8 million scam calls. [...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Forg365 PhaaS Targets Microsoft 365 with Device Code and AitM Session Theft
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 is using a combination of device code phishing, adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) tactics, antibot evasion, artificial intelligence (AI)-assis...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Turning the Tables on Email Scammers With 'ScamBuster'
item.description: An open source, AI-driven system adopts victim personas to engage with phishing attackers, allowing organizations and law enforcement to gather relevant data on cybercriminal operations....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Meta Files Patent for AI That Can Listen All Day and Track How You're Feeling
item.description: Meta has filed a patent application for an AI that listens to your voice throughout the day, works out how it thinks you are feeling from the way you sound, and keeps a timestamped log of every read. ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Thinking Fast and Slow in the SOC: The Case for Combining Autonomous AI with Analyst Copilots
item.description: A few days ago, I was sitting with the CISO of a Fortune 50 company, walking through how his security team was thinking about AI agents in the SOC. Smart team. Serious program. They had already connec...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: EU sanctions Russian GRU military hackers over cyberattacks
item.description: The European Union and the United Kingdom jointly sanctioned dozens of Russian individuals and entities and accused Russia of coordinating a network of hacking groups responsible for attacks across Eu...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Attacker Uses Suspected AI-Generated PowerShell Script to Map Active Directory
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an intrusion in which an unknown threat actor leveraged a vibe-coded PowerShell script for Active Directory (AD) enumeration.
"The script looked for the Domain ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US and allies warn of Russian critical infrastructure attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity agencies from the United States and eight other countries have issued a joint warning that Russian state hackers are targeting vulnerable and poorly configured routers to infiltrate crit...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Misconfigured Server Reveals Three Evilginx Phishing Operations Targeting Microsoft 365
item.description: An attacker running a live Microsoft 365 phishing operation left a Python web server listening on a public port with directory listing switched on. The command that did it: python3 -m http.server 8080...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: iCagenda and Balbooa Forms Joomla Flaws Reportedly Exploited as Zero-Days
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added two maximum-severity security flaws impacting iCagenda and Balbooa extensions for Joomla to its Known Exploited Vulnerabiliti...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI temporarily relaxes GPT-5.6 Sol usage limits
item.description: OpenAI is temporarily relaxing GPT-5.6 Sol usage after demand for the company's most powerful model surged over the past 48 hours. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Claude Fable 5 stays free for paid users until July 19 as Anthropic buys more time
item.description: Anthropic has just extended access to Claude Fable 5 for paid subscribers until July 19, giving you another week to keep using the most powerful model. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: RedHook Android malware now uses Wireless ADB for shell access
item.description: A new version of the RedHook Android malware abuses the Android Wireless Debugging (Wireless ADB) mechanism in a novel way to gain shell-level privileges without requiring a computer connection. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Compromised jscrambler 8.14.0 npm Release Drops Rust Infostealer During Install
item.description: The jscrambler npm package was compromised, and simply installing its 8.14.0 release runs an infostealer on your machine. Published on July 11, 2026, the malicious version carries a preinstall hook th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers Weaponize Balochistan Police Portal in Multi-Group Espionage Campaigns
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of sustained cyber espionage activity against several Pakistani law enforcement organizations undertaken by suspected China- and India-aligned threat a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Australia warns of global campaign targeting vulnerable CMS platforms
item.description: The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) issued an alert about a global exploitation campaign targeting vulnerable content management systems (CMS) and plugins. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'Ghostcommit' hides prompt injection in images to fool AI agents, steal secrets
item.description: A PNG hiding a prompt injection could steal your repo's secrets, researchers demonstrate. The technique, dubbed 'Ghostcommit,' slipped past AI code reviewers CodeRabbit and Bugbot, which never open im...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical Zimbra Flaw Could Let Crafted Emails Run Malicious Code in User Sessions
item.description:
Zimbra is urging customers to apply updates to address a critical security vulnerability impacting the Classic Web Client that could result in arbitrary code execution.
The vulnerability has been des...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New U-Boot flaws could enable stealthy firmware attacks
item.description: Six vulnerabilities in the widely used U-Boot bootloader have been discovered that could allow attackers to execute malicious code during device boot, potentially enabling stealthy firmware attacks th...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Jen Ellis: Connecting Cyber Community With Political Machinery
item.description: Security Pro File: On the heels of her recent honors as a Member of the Order of the British Empire (MBE), we take a look back at the events that shaped Jen Ellis' advocacy on behalf of security resea...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Ryuk ransomware member pleads guilty in the US, faces 15 years in prison
item.description: A 34-year-old Armenian man has pleaded guilty to hacking U.S. companies and deploying the infamous Ryuk ransomware to encrypt their systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cybercriminals Flock to Healthcare Businesses as Attacks Surge
item.description: While cyberattacks against hospitals and clinics grew modestly in the first half of 2026, attacks on service providers and other healthcare businesses more than doubled....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Police suspects Dutch hackers were involved in Odido breach
item.description: The Dutch National Police (Politie) says it has found "strong indications" that Dutch hackers have been involved in a February breach at the telecommunications provider Odido. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: URGENT - Progress Tells ShareFile Customers to Shut Down Storage Zone Controllers Over Security Threat
item.description: Progress Software has told ShareFile customers to shut down the Windows servers running their Storage Zone Controllers, confirming to The Hacker News that it is responding to a "credible external secu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Injective Labs GitHub Compromise Pushes Wallet-Key-Stealing npm Packages
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and leveraged it to publish a malicious package on the npm registry to steal cryptocurrency wallet private keys and...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Progress urges ShareFile admins to shut down servers over “credible” threat
item.description: Progress Software is emailing ShareFile customers who use Storage Zone Controllers to immediately shut down their servers after identifying what it describes as a "credible external security threat" t...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
item.description: Researchers at firmware security firm Binarly have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips inside da...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers exploit critical auth bypass in Gitea Docker image
item.description: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the official Docker image for the Gitea self-hosted Git service that allows attackers to impersonate any user, including administrators. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
